message:
Implement real fsck --force semantics with clean-state skip logic

Add ext4_appears_clean_state() to check if filesystem state flags
indicate a clean volume (VALID_FS set, ERROR_FS and ORPHAN_FS clear).
When the filesystem appears clean and --force is not set, skip the
expensive block-level checksum scrub and report zero blocks scanned
with a clear explanation message.

Changes to build_fsck_output:
- Add skip_full_scrub decision based on clean state + no --force flag
- Insert new branch that emits zero-count scrub report with detail
  string explaining the skip and how to override with --force
- Thread scrub_skipped_detail through the return tuple for all
  filesystem flavors (ext4 and btrfs)
- Add limitation entry when scrub is skipped for transparency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
